title: The 21st Century Action Hero Hall of Fame
format: Uncharted has Sean wondering what an action hero even is anymore, so he calls in Shea Serrano to build a Hall of Fame for the post-Schwarzenegger era. Ten spots, two hosts, a lot of casualties.

segments.heading: Uncharted and the state of action movies

films.title: Uncharted
films.note: The new Tom Holland/Mark Wahlberg video-game adaptation that kicked off the conversation — Sean calls it a relic of an older kind of movie stardom.

films.title: Red Notice
films.note: Shea's example of the old model failing: a movie built to be the biggest thing on the planet that barely registered. He liked it in the moment and forgot it in seven minutes.

films.title: The Night Comes for Us
films.note: The counter-example — a smaller, more potent Netflix action movie that fans actively sought out, unlike Red Notice.

segments.heading: Are superhero movies action movies?
segments.note: Sean and Shea agree to disagree

films.title: The Batman
films.note: Sean's test case for the genre line — a grounded, alien-free Batman movie that Shea still wouldn't call an action film, since 'we have watched Batman fight aliens' elsewhere.

films.title: The Raid
films.note: The credibility voucher — Shea's shorthand for movie-nerd coolness, the equivalent of name-dropping MF DOOM to a rap fan.

films.title: The Raid 2
films.note: The kitchen fight scene Shea cites as an instant-friendship test.

films.title: Snake Eyes
films.note: The G.I. Joe spinoff where Uwais plays Hard Master and barely gets to fight — Shea's recurring complaint about how Hollywood wastes him.

films.title: Mile 22
films.note: Another example of the waste: he's handcuffed to a hospital bed for the big fight scene, chopped into a hundred quick cuts instead of let loose.

films.title: Fistful of Vengeance
films.note: New on Netflix that week. Sean calls it fun, not up to Raid standards.

films.title: The Rundown
films.note: One of the cherry-picked best-of titles Sean cites for his top-level case.

films.title: Walking Tall
films.note: Another of the pre-Fast best-of picks.

films.title: Fast Five
films.note: Called an all-time great and the closest thing Johnson has to a signature, career-defining movie — though it's the fifth film in a franchise he joined late.

films.title: Journey 2: The Mysterious Island
films.note: A family favorite in Shea's house, Friday movie-night material.

films.title: Snitch
films.note: The start of Johnson's five-movies-in-one-year stretch. Shea calls it a nifty little crime thriller.

films.title: G.I. Joe: Retaliation
films.note: Thumbs down from both hosts.

films.title: Pain & Gain
films.note: The controversial one. Both hosts like it.

films.title: Fast & Furious 6
films.note: Part of the 2013 run.

films.title: Empire State
films.note: The direct-to-video entry from that same stretch that neither host has seen.

films.title: San Andreas
films.note: Sean saw it alone in an LA theater and clocked the moment Johnson actually tries to act — the helicopter scene with his ex-wife — and how the room just waited for the next set piece instead of feeling anything.

films.title: Baywatch
films.note: Called funny, not good.

films.title: Rampage
films.note: Bad, per both hosts.

films.title: Skyscraper
films.note: Also bad — 'they ran out of stuff for you to fight... so he fought a building.'

films.title: Fast & Furious Presents: Hobbs & Shaw
films.note: Not great, but Sean likes the bit where he yanks a helicopter out of the sky with a chain.

films.title: Snatch
films.note: One of his Guy Ritchie credits.

films.title: The Italian Job
films.note: Called a big one for him.

films.title: The Transporter
films.note: The franchise that anchors his case.

films.title: Crank
films.note: Shea's headline argument for inclusion.

films.title: Crank: High Voltage
films.note: 'The most Statham movie that's ever been,' per Shea.

films.title: The Bank Job
films.note: Named as part of his resume.

films.title: The Mechanic
films.note: A favorite pairing with Ben Foster, per Sean.

films.title: Parker
films.note: One of his attempts at a franchise lead.

films.title: Spy
films.note: Called hilarious as an over-eager spy.

films.title: The Meg
films.note: Sean says it's one of the only movies he's wanted to walk out of; still went and paid for the IMAX upgrade for his family.

films.title: Wrath of Man
films.note: Referenced as another Guy Ritchie collaboration — the score does more work than the movie, per Sean.

films.title: Fast & Furious Presents: Hobbs & Shaw
films.note: Also part of his resume, alongside Johnson's.

films.title: The Expendables
films.note: Cited in passing as part of his action pedigree.

segments.heading: Charlize Theron
segments.note: 'fight confidence,' the term Shea coins for her

films.title: Æon Flux
films.note: Flagged by Sean as an early, less-successful example of her doing physical work.

films.title: Hancock
films.note: Where she first proved she could do the wire work, per Shea — though Sean flags it as arguably a superhero movie.

films.title: Mad Max: Fury Road
films.note: The fight with Tom Hardy that convinced Shea she was for real.

films.title: Atomic Blonde
films.note: The hotel fight scene both hosts cite as her clearest action-star case.

segments.heading: Liam Neeson
segments.note: the flashpoint for the whole 'older guy with a particular set of skills' era

films.title: Taken
films.note: The movie that made 'Liam Neeson action star' a sentence people said. Not a great fighter, per Shea, but carries scenes on pure gravity.

films.title: The Grey
films.note: Called one of his best, alongside Taken.

films.title: Non-Stop
films.note: Sean's favorite of the run — the one on the plane.

films.title: Blacklight
films.note: His then-new release, noted as apparently not very good.

films.title: Spawn
films.note: Cited as proof he can do the superhero thing too.

films.title: The Dark Knight
films.note: His scene became a meme, per Shea — mentioned as evidence of his reach beyond niche action.

films.title: Black Dynamite
films.note: The role that revealed the comedic range nobody knew he had.

films.title: Undisputed II: Last Man Standing
films.note: The one Shea says people need to watch before they mock this pick.

films.title: Never Back Down
films.note: Named as one of his spotty-but-reliable direct-to-video run.

films.title: Never Back Down: No Surrender
films.note: Another entry in that same run.

films.title: Undercover Brother 2
films.note: A sequel Sean didn't know existed, and White wasn't even the original lead.

segments.heading: Tony Jaa
segments.note: in on the strength of Ong-Bak alone

films.title: Ong-Bak
films.note: The signature franchise Shea says is enough by itself.

films.title: Furious 7
films.note: His U.S. breakout, fighting Paul Walker on a bus.

films.title: xXx: Return of Xander Cage
films.note: Part of his run of American productions after Furious 7.

films.title: Master Z: Ip Man Legacy
films.note: Also cited from that stretch.

films.title: Detective Chinatown 3
films.note: Noted as barely released stateside but the highest-grossing R-rated movie in Chinese box-office history, at roughly $700 million.

films.title: Monster Hunter
films.note: Watched by Sean with his youngest son; both hosts call it not great.

segments.heading: Michelle Rodriguez
segments.note: swapped in for Vin Diesel — 'she does what he does, but better'

films.title: Girlfight
films.note: Shea's been Team Rodriguez since this one, per his own past writing calling her the secret MVP of the Fast franchise.

films.title: Fast Five
films.note: The franchise entry that anchors her case, same as Johnson's.

segments.heading: Donnie Yen
segments.note: Shea's personal favorite of the group

films.title: Ip Man
films.note: The series Shea calls 'so good,' and the source of the rapid-punch move he nicknames the Gatling gun.

films.title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story
films.note: Both hosts like how the film leans into his calm, blind-warrior stillness rather than flashy choreography.

segments.heading: Matt Damon
segments.note: the last spot, and the only white American on the final list

films.title: The Bourne Supremacy
films.note: The handheld, fast-camera style Sean credits as a genuine marking moment for the genre — the reason Shea ultimately can't leave him off.

films.title: Equilibrium
films.note: Christian Bale's case for inclusion, and Shea's exhibit A against him — 'you look like you've never held a weapon.' Bale is out.

films.title: 3:10 to Yuma
films.note: Bale's western credential, conceded as great but not action.

films.title: Reign of Fire
films.note: Sean's counter-argument for Bale, though Shea notes the trailer's iconic shot went to Matthew McConaughey instead.

films.title: Her
films.note: Shea's joke nomination for Scarlett Johansson before making a real case for her. She's out.

films.title: The Island
films.note: Cited as part of Johansson's actual action filmography.

films.title: Lucy
films.note: Sean's pick for her — Shea says he'd 'karate kick him through the screen' for suggesting it. He doesn't like it.

films.title: U.S. Marshals
films.note: Sean's case for Robert Downey Jr.; Shea says John Cusack would've been better in the role. RDJ is out.

films.title: Sherlock Holmes
films.note: Downey Jr.'s stronger case, but Sean notes he'd need more than two of them.

films.title: Dolittle
films.note: Sean's last, failed pitch for Downey Jr. — 'the only movie that ever made me dislike animals.'

films.title: Wanted
films.note: Part of Angelina Jolie's case for inclusion.

films.title: Salt
films.note: Also cited for Jolie.

films.title: Lara Croft: Tomb Raider
films.note: Called fitting, since the whole episode started with Uncharted.

films.title: Gone in Sixty Seconds
films.note: Noted along with Charlize Theron's Fury Road as the two hosts trade a bit about actresses with dreadlocks in movies.

films.title: Eternals
films.note: Jolie's recent superhero credit — both hosts admit they didn't fully understand the movie, but liked her fight sequence.

films.title: Van Helsing
films.note: Hugh Jackman's action case. Shea has watched the commentary track repeatedly but hasn't seen the actual movie.

films.title: Real Steel
films.note: Called underrated by Sean, but not enough on its own.

films.title: Safe House
films.note: Ryan Reynolds's grittier credit, opposite Denzel Washington.

films.title: Smokin' Aces
films.note: Called Reynolds's best lane by Sean.

films.title: Free Guy
films.note: Sean's family liked it; Shea calls it terrible.

films.title: Cowboys & Aliens
films.note: Daniel Craig's non-Bond action credit, called not super successful but fun.

films.title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
films.note: Named as one of Craig's dramas with action elements, not a true action movie.

films.title: Defiance
films.note: Also cited in that category for Craig, who's out despite a 'soft yes.'

films.title: Death on the Nile
films.note: Gal Gadot's poorly received new release, cited as a sign 'the bloom is off the rose' for her.

films.title: Date Night
films.note: Cited as an actual action role for Gadot, pre-Wonder Woman.

films.title: Knight and Day
films.note: Also cited for Gadot's pre-superhero action work.

films.title: Extraction
films.note: Chris Hemsworth's strongest action credit, called 'a perfect example' — but not enough on its own. He's out for now.

films.title: Blackhat
films.note: Hemsworth as a physically imposing hacker.

films.title: Star Trek
films.note: Noted as Hemsworth playing Kirk's father.

films.title: Spiderhead
films.note: One of the three upcoming Hemsworth films the hosts say could tip him into the Hall next time.

films.title: Extraction 2
films.note: The sequel both hosts are looking forward to.

films.title: Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga
films.note: The Mad Max prequel, then upcoming, cited as Hemsworth's biggest swing yet.

films.title: Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon
films.note: Zhang Ziyi's headline credit, deemed a launch-year borderline case for the 2000 cutoff.

films.title: Hero
films.note: Also cited for Zhang Ziyi.

films.title: House of Flying Daggers
films.note: Also cited for Zhang Ziyi.

films.title: The Grandmaster
films.note: Also cited for Zhang Ziyi, who ultimately doesn't make the final ten.

films.title: Undisputed II: Last Man Standing
films.note: Scott Adkins's breakout as the heavy opposite Michael Jai White — the movie that got him his own spinoff.

films.title: Universal Soldier: Day of Reckoning
films.note: Sean's first exposure to Adkins, alongside Jean-Claude Van Damme and Dolph Lundgren.

films.title: Unleashed
films.note: Adkins's fight scene opposite Jet Li that Sean sought out afterward.

films.title: Bloodshot
films.note: Vin Diesel's cited credit, before he's bumped from the list in favor of Michelle Rodriguez.

films.title: Saving Private Ryan
films.note: Also cited for Diesel.

films.title: The Iron Giant
films.note: Diesel's voice role, cited in passing.

films.title: The Pacifier
films.note: Also cited for Diesel.

films.title: Resident Evil
films.note: Milla Jovovich's signature franchise, called dependable B-grade action. She's out.

films.title: Ultraviolet
films.note: Also cited for Jovovich.

films.title: Monster Hunter
films.note: Also cited for Jovovich, who is married to director Paul W.S. Anderson.

referenced.group: The pre-2000s stars, ruled ineligible from the jump

Unreleased: Mortal Kombat (leaked fan test footage with Michael Jai White as Jax, never made)

Rogue One: A Star Wars Story.title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story

The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo.title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
